articles.title: Cambridge City Council votes to end use of ShotSpotter technology
articles.description:
ShotSpotter deploys a network of acoustic sensors that pick up the sounds of gunshots, but critics say it is unreliable and leads to the over-policing of communities of color.

The post Cambridge City Council votes to end use of ShotSpotter technology appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Cambridge City Council voted to end ShotSpotter technology on May 20, 2026.
Critics argue ShotSpotter leads to over-policing in communities of color.
Residents may experience reduced surveillance and potential changes in police response.

articles.title: Grand jury indicts Boston police officer on charge of manslaughter in fatal shooting
articles.description:
Nicholas O'Malley is the first Boston officer to be charged in a fatal on-duty shooting in decades.

The post Grand jury indicts Boston police officer on charge of manslaughter in fatal shooting appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Nicholas O'Malley indicted for manslaughter in a fatal shooting incident.
This marks the first indictment of a Boston officer for on-duty shooting in decades.
The case raises concerns about police accountability and community safety in Boston.

articles.title: How hot are Boston classrooms today? See temperatures reported across BPS schools.
articles.description:
Charlestown High School recorded the highest average temperature at 84.59 degrees.

The post How hot are Boston classrooms today? See temperatures reported across BPS schools. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Charlestown High School reported the highest average classroom temperature at 84.59 degrees.
Many Boston Public Schools are experiencing uncomfortably high temperatures this week.
Students and teachers may face challenges in learning and concentration due to heat.

articles.title: Harvard caps A’s as selective colleges attack grade inflation
articles.description:
The new policy will limit A’s to 20% of the letter grades awarded in a course, with an allowance for as many as four additional A’s.

The post Harvard caps A’s as selective colleges attack grade inflation appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Harvard University limits A's to 20% of grades per course effective immediately.
Students can receive up to four additional A's under the new grading policy.
This change aims to address grade inflation, impacting local students' academic evaluations.

articles.title: ‘I stink at fantasy football’: Fan at Red Sox game goes viral with creative punishment
articles.description:
"They're not even allowing him to have a beverage."

The post ‘I stink at fantasy football’: Fan at Red Sox game goes viral with creative punishment appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Fan at Red Sox game humorously admits poor fantasy football skills with a sign.
The fan's creative punishment includes not being allowed to drink beverages.
Viral moment highlights local sports culture and community engagement in Boston.

articles.title: Tennessee man jailed over Charlie Kirk post wins $835,000 settlement
articles.description:
Larry Bushart was arrested in September after he refused to take down Facebook memes that joked about Kirk’s killing, which had prompted an outpouring of grief among conservatives

The post Tennessee man jailed over Charlie Kirk post wins $835,000 settlement appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Larry Bushart was jailed in September for refusing to remove Facebook memes about Charlie Kirk.
Bushart received an $835,000 settlement after his arrest for the controversial post.
This case raises concerns about free speech and social media policies affecting Boston residents.

articles.title: Rosebud in Somerville is closed, but just for renovations
articles.description:
After rumors spread that the landmark diner was shutting down for good, the owner says the closure is only temporary.

The post Rosebud in Somerville is closed, but just for renovations appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Rosebud diner in Somerville is temporarily closed for renovations.
Owner confirms closure is not permanent, alleviating community concerns.
Local residents can expect renovations to enhance dining experience.

articles.title: ‘Infuriation’: Animal control reports rescuing puppy from 100-degree car at Legacy Place
articles.description:
The puppy's owners were shopping at the Dedham mall, officials say.

The post ‘Infuriation’: Animal control reports rescuing puppy from 100-degree car at Legacy Place appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Animal control rescued a puppy left in a 100-degree car at Legacy Place.
The puppy's owners were shopping at the Dedham mall during the incident.
This incident highlights the dangers of leaving pets in hot vehicles for Boston residents.

articles.title: Boston College baseball, coming off one of its best seasons ever, enters ACC Tournament with big hopes
articles.description:
The Eagles finished fourth in the conference.

The post Boston College baseball, coming off one of its best seasons ever, enters ACC Tournament with big hopes appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston College baseball finished fourth in the ACC, marking a strong season.
The Eagles enter the ACC Tournament with high hopes for success.
Local fans anticipate increased support and excitement during the tournament.
